chunks.text: Ali Niknejad is a Professor at UC Berkeley's EECS department, holding the Donald O. Pederson Distinguished Professorship. He serves as faculty co-director of the Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC), Associate Director of the Center for Ubiquitous Connectivity (CUbiC), and previously as Associate Director for the Center for Converged TeraHertz Communications and Sensing (ComSenTer). He received his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from UC Berkeley in 2000.
chunks.question: What positions does Ali Niknejad hold at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Professor and faculty co-director of BWRC.

chunks.text: Ali Niknejad's research interests include wireless and broadband communications, biomedical imaging and sensors, integrated circuit technology (analog, RF, mixed-signal, mm-wave), device physics and compact modeling, and applied electromagnetics. His research areas encompass Integrated Circuits, Microwave and mm-Wave Circuits and Systems, Physical Electronics, Signal Processing, Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences, Design, Modeling and Analysis, and Applied Electromagnetics.
chunks.question: What are Ali Niknejad's main research interests?

chunks.text: Ali Niknejad received the 2020 SIA/SRC University Research Award for advancing analog, RF, and mm-wave circuit design, foundational to 5G+ technologies. He also won the 2017 IEEE Transactions On Circuits And Systems Darlington Best Paper Award, the 2017 Most Frequently Cited Paper Award of the Symposium on Very Large-Scale Integration Circuits, the CICC 2015 Best Invited Paper Award, and the 2012 ASEE Frederick Emmons Terman Award.
chunks.question: What award did Ali Niknejad receive in 2020?

chunks.text: Ali Niknejad's education includes a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from UC Berkeley in 2000, an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from UC Berkeley in 1997, and a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from UCLA in 1994. He teaches courses such as EE 142 and EE 242A, Integrated Circuits for Communications, held in Cory 247 on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 11:00-12:29.
chunks.question: Where did Ali Niknejad earn his Ph.D.?

chunks.text: Selected publications by Ali Niknejad include works on low-power receivers for IoT, RF-powered-tag intermodulation, wideband CMOS transmitters, and E-band QPSK transmitters. Notable papers involve collaborations on topics like mutual noise-canceling receivers, inductive power transfer, and gain boosting arrays for wireless power transfer, published in journals such as IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques.
chunks.question: What is a focus of Ali Niknejad's publications?

chunks.text: Ali Niknejad has received honors including the SSCS Innovative Education Award in 2024, the SIA University Research Award in 2020, the IEEE CASS Darlington Best Paper Award in 2017, and being named an IEEE Fellow in 2012. He also co-received the Jack Kilby Award for Outstanding Student Paper in 2013 and 2010, and the Outstanding Technology Directions Paper at ISSCC 2004.
chunks.question: When was Ali Niknejad named an IEEE Fellow?
